mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflect expanding manufacturing output, which directly boosts corporate earnings and signals economic strength, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not consistently positive (Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 6.5% after a period of high unemployment, it signals tightening labor market conditions that may lead to wage inflation and preemptive Federal Reserve tightening, which depresses equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE declines below 6.5%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is positive and significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts (HOUST above 800k) and retail sales (RSAFS above 350k) indicates broad-based consumer demand that sustains economic growth and lifts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in instances where both HOUST > 800 and RSAFS > 350 hold, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or if the joint indicator does not outperform a linear combination of the two individual series, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound regime (FEDFUNDS near zero), any increase in the federal funds rate surprises markets as a signal of premature tightening, which creates negative repricing of risk assets and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during ZLB periods, FEDFUNDS increases (clearly > 0.25%) are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 50% of events over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures (proxied by RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a surge in consumer activity that immediately boosts market sentiment and leads to positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after RSAFS MoM > 1%, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k signal a persistently improving labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS gains above 100k and 60-day forward SPX returns is negative or insignificant (Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When short-term interest rates are low (FEDFUNDS below 1%) and inflation expectations rise (CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 3%), the combination signals a negative supply shock or overheating that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin concerns, leading to negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: low rate environment with incipient inflation
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL yoy > 3%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive and not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: During expansion phases when real GDP growth (GDPC1 year-over-year) exceeds 2.5%, further positive GDP surprises reinforce the cyclical uptrend and corporate earnings momentum, leading to above-average S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This positive relationship does not hold in contractions or near peaks.
failure_condition: If during expansion phases (GDPC1 yoy > 2.5%), the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive and does not exceed non-expansion phase returns by at least 1% average, reject.
